description: Specification for Cube Baby virtual bank presets. Documents factory amp models, IR cabinet profiles, working limits, logic, and constraints for generating valid presets.

amp_models.description: 9 factory Amp Preamp models. The Type knob cycles through these models covering everything from classic clean tones to high-gain modern distortion.

0.name: US Clean
0.sonic_characteristics: Transparent, high-headroom clean tone with a bright top end.

1.name: UK Clean
1.sonic_characteristics: Classic British chime with warm, mid-focused bite when pushed.

2.name: US Blues
2.sonic_characteristics: Edge-of-breakup grit that responds well to playing dynamics.

3.name: UK Crunch
3.sonic_characteristics: Classic rock crunch with a prominent midrange push.

4.name: US Gold 100
4.sonic_characteristics: Modern, saturated British brown sound with tighter low end.

5.name: Cali JP A
5.amp_lineage: Mesa Boogie JP-2C / Mark Series style
5.sonic_characteristics: Thick liquid lead tone, ideal for articulate soloing.

6.name: German High-Gain
6.sonic_characteristics: Deep, heavy high-gain tone with clear note separation for metal rhythm.

7.name: US Modern High-Gain
7.sonic_characteristics: Aggressive, scooped-mid high-gain with massive low-end wallop.

8.name: UK Modern
8.sonic_characteristics: Fuzzy, dark, thick high-gain distortion with unique mid harmonics.

ir_cabinets.description: 9 factory Impulse Response (IR) Cabinet profiles (positions 0-8). Position 0 is a DI bypass (no simulation, flat response). Positions 1 through 8 are captured cabinet configurations.

0.name: DI Box
0.sonic_characteristics: Flat response; useful for bypass or acoustic patches.

1.name: Line 6 Vetta 1x12
1.sonic_characteristics: Compact, punchy, relatively flat mid-range response.

2.name: Marshall 1960AV
2.sonic_characteristics: Mid-forward, aggressive projection; the rock/metal standard.

3.name: Marshall 1960A T75
3.sonic_characteristics: Slightly scooped mids with bright highs and deep bass.

4.name: VHT Deliverance 2x12
4.sonic_characteristics: Exceptionally tight low-end tracking; perfect for staccato riffing.

5.name: Soldano 2x12
5.sonic_characteristics: Smooth, warm compression; great for fluid lead sounds.

6.name: Peavey 5150 + Mesa Oversized 4x12
6.sonic_characteristics: Massive wall of sound; heavy low-end emphasis for metalcore/deathcore.

7.name: JSX KT77 + Mesa Stiletto 4x12
7.sonic_characteristics: Articulate, mid-detailed tone with a crisp high-end response.

8.name: Diezel V30 4x12
8.sonic_characteristics: Aggressive upper-mid cut with a modern, focused distortion frame.

section_layout.description: The Cube Baby DSP is divided into three hardware sections. Each section can be bypassed independently via its flag.

A: IR.note: When irSection is false, reverb and cabinet are bypassed regardless of their values.

B: Modulation+Delay.note: Despite the flag name 'delaySection', it controls the ENTIRE modulation+delay block. Setting delaySection=false bypasses both chorus/phaser AND delay. If mod!==7 (chorus/phaser active) OR mix>0 (delay active), delaySection MUST be true or those effects will be inaudible.

C: Tone.note: When toneSection is false, the amplifier model is bypassed.

format.description: The format identifier for virtual bank files. Must be exactly this string.

id.description: Unique preset identifier. Format: 'vp_' followed by a positive integer (e.g. 'vp_1', 'vp_42'). Must be unique within the file. Leading zeros are valid but not generated by the app.

name.description: Human-readable preset name shown in the UI.

knobs.description: All knob parameter values and section flags (see parameter_definitions below).

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the preset was first created.

updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the preset was last modified.

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bank file was first generated/exported.

updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last bank modification (optional; the export code does not write this field, but imported banks may include it).

critical_rules: MODULATION AND DELAY MUST HAVE delaySection=true: If knobValues.mod !== 7 (meaning chorus or phaser is active) OR knobValues.mix > 0 (delay is on), delaySection MUST be set to true. Otherwise the entire modulation+delay block is bypassed and those effects will be silent.
delaySection FALSE is only safe when BOTH mod===7 AND mix===0: It is acceptable to set delaySection=false only when there is no modulation effect (mod=7) and no delay (mix=0).
Mutual exclusion: chorus and phaser cannot be active simultaneously. mod values 0-6 indicate chorus, 8-15 indicate phaser, 7 is neutral (no modulation).
Modulation values 0-6 and 9-15 are always valid for delaySection=true regardless of mix value: you can have chorus OR phaser active without delay (mix=0) as long as delaySection=true.

type.description: The core amplifier/effect model type. Maps to entries in the amp_models lookup table.

gain.description: Amount of distortion or input level.

mod.description: Modulation type and intensity. 0-6 = Chorus (0=most intense → 6=least intense, inverted scale). 7 = Off/neutral. 8-15 = Phaser (8=least intense → 15=most intense, direct scale). Chorus and phaser are mutually exclusive — 7 is the only value for no modulation.

time.description: Delay time step index (or modulation speed when mod is active). Range 0-31 is a hardware step index, not milliseconds.
time.note: When delay pedal is toggled OFF in live mode, time and feedback are preserved (not zeroed). Only mix is set to 0. Values 0-31 are hardware step indices — no ms↔index mapping is documented.

fb.description: Delay feedback amount (number of repeats). Higher values = more repeats. Only audible when mix > 0 (delay active).

mix.description: Wet/Dry mix for the delay effect. 0 = no delay (muted), max = full wet. Max 118 is a hardware-imposed limit (not 127 like most other knobs).
mix.note: Delay pedal ON/OFF is controlled by mix only: 0=OFF, saved value=ON. Time and feedback are not touched when toggling delay. Values 0 and 118 are hardware limits.

reverb.description: Reverb decay/size. Requires irSection=true to be heard.

ir_cab.description: Impulse Response Cabinet selection. Maps to entries in the ir_cabinets lookup table. Requires irSection=true to be heard.

volume.description: Output volume level (always active, not stompable).

irSection.description: Enable/Disable the IR section (reverb + cabinet). When false, reverb and cabinet are bypassed.

delaySection.description: Enable/Disable the modulation+delay block (Section B). When false, BOTH chorus/phaser AND delay are bypassed. This flag is automatically corrected by knobValuesToSettings() if mod!==7 or mix>0, but presets should store the correct value.
delaySection.must_be_true_when: mod !== 7 (chorus or phaser active) OR mix > 0 (delay active)

toneSection.description: Enable/Disable the tone section (amp type, gain, tone). When false, the amplifier is bypassed.

generator_checklist: Set delaySection to true when mod !== 7 OR mix > 0. Only set delaySection=false when mod===7 AND mix===0 simultaneously.
mod=7 is the only neutral/off value for modulation. Values 0-6 are chorus (inverted: 0=most intense, 6=least). Values 8-15 are phaser (direct: 8=least, 15=most).
ir_cab range is 0-8 (9 entries). Position 0 is DI bypass (flat response, no simulation).
fb (feedback) is only audible when mix > 0 (delay active). When mix=0, fb can be any value 0-127 but will not be heard.
time range is 0-31. These are hardware step indices, not milliseconds. No ms↔index mapping is documented.
mix max is 118, not 127. This is a hardware-imposed limit.
id must follow 'vp_<integer>' pattern (e.g. 'vp_1'). Leading zeros are allowed but not generated by the app.
Bank format string must be exactly 'cubebabyvirtualbank' (lowercase, no spaces).
